rationale: The previous attempt asserted the ensures-foralls as if they were free hypotheses, but they are the antecedent of an implication in the ensures, so the asserts failed. Wrap the proof in `if antecedent { ... }` so each conjunct (including both `is_None` foralls and both `wf()`s) becomes a hypothesis inside the branch; then use `assert forall ... implies ... by { ... }` to instantiate the two disjoint triggers `post1_self_@[i]` and `post2_self_@[i]` at a common `i`, and close with Seq extensionality `=~=`. After the `if`, Verus has `antecedent ==> post1_self_@ == post2_self_@`, which (combined with `r1 == r2` being trivial for units) is what `det_init2none_equal` needs at the Seq-view level.
